name: Rohini
shortDescription: A lunar goddess and the most beloved wife of Chandra, the moon god, celebrated for her beauty and auspicious nature.
description: Rohini, in Hindu mythology, is a prominent lunar goddess and one of the twenty-seven daughters of Daksha, all of whom were married to Chandra, the moon god. She is consistently depicted as Chandra's favorite wife, a distinction that often led to jealousy among her sisters. Rohini is often associated with fertility, growth, and auspiciousness. Her name, derived from the Sanskrit word meaning "reddish" or "growing," reflects her connection to creation and abundance. In astrological contexts, Rohini Nakshatra (lunar mansion) is considered one of the most favorable, promising prosperity and good fortune. Stories involving Rohini primarily revolve around Chandra's favoritism and the consequences that followed, showcasing themes of jealousy, divine curses, and the cyclical nature of time and karma.
subtitle: The radiant consort of the Moon.
role: Goddess of fertility, prosperity, and a lunar consort

extendedContent.title: Origins & Mythology
extendedContent.content: Rohini's origin is traced back to the lineage of Daksha, a Prajapati (creator deity). Daksha's twenty-seven daughters were married to Chandra, symbolizing the moon's monthly cycle through the Nakshatras. However, Chandra showed particular affection for Rohini, spending most of his time with her and neglecting his other wives. This blatant favoritism angered Daksha, who initially warned Chandra and then, upon repeated offenses, cursed him to wane. The curse led to Chandra's decline, affecting the earth's fertility. Eventually, through prayers and penance, Chandra was partially relieved of the curse, resulting in the waxing and waning phases of the moon. Rohini remained a steadfast and beloved consort throughout these events.

extendedContent.title: Role & Significance
extendedContent.content: Rohini holds significant importance in Hindu astrology and religious practices. The Rohini Nakshatra is considered highly auspicious for initiating new ventures, marriages, and other important life events. She is often invoked for blessings of fertility, prosperity, and overall well-being. Her association with Chandra reinforces her role as a lunar deity, influencing the tides, agriculture, and the emotional state of individuals. Temples dedicated to Chandra often feature Rohini as his consort, emphasizing their divine partnership. Worshipping Rohini is believed to bring harmony in marital life and bless families with healthy offspring.

extendedContent.title: Symbolism & Interpretation
extendedContent.content: Rohini symbolizes beauty, love, and growth. Her reddish complexion, implied by her name, represents passion and vitality. The Rohini Nakshatra, associated with her, is symbolized by a chariot or a banyan tree, representing progress, stability, and nourishment. The story of Chandra's favoritism towards Rohini can be interpreted as a metaphor for the importance of balance and fairness in relationships. The curse and subsequent relief represent the cyclical nature of life, the consequences of actions, and the power of devotion. Rohini's enduring love for Chandra symbolizes loyalty and perseverance in the face of adversity.

extendedContent.title: Legacy & Influence
extendedContent.content: Rohini continues to be revered in contemporary Hindu society, particularly in astrological contexts. Her Nakshatra remains a significant factor in determining auspicious timings for various activities. The story of Rohini and Chandra serves as a reminder of the importance of equitable treatment and the consequences of imbalance. Her image and name are still used in various forms of art, literature, and popular culture, reinforcing her enduring presence in the Hindu pantheon. Additionally, many Hindu women are named Rohini, as a tribute to the goddess's beauty and auspiciousness.

sources.author: Vettam Mani
sources.description: A comprehensive dictionary of Hindu Puranic lore, including details on Rohini's role and stories.

sources.author: Roshen Dalal
sources.description: An accessible guide to Hindu concepts and figures, with a concise entry on Rohini.

associations.name: Moon
associations.significance: She is the consort of the moon god Chandra.

associations.name: Fertility
associations.significance: She is associated with fertility and abundance.

associations.name: Red
associations.significance: Her name means reddish, symbolizing passion and vitality.

cross_cultural_parallels.name: Selene
cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: A lunar goddess associated with the moon, similar to Rohini's connection to Chandra.

cross_cultural_parallels.name: Chang'e
cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: A lunar deity residing on the moon, sharing a connection to lunar symbolism with Rohini.

companions.name: Chandra
companions.description: The moon god, and Rohini's beloved husband.

companions.name: Daksha
companions.description: Father of Rohini and the other Nakshatra goddesses.

worshipPractices.name: Rohini Vrat
worshipPractices.description: A fast observed on the day Rohini Nakshatra prevails, seeking blessings for family welfare and prosperity.

worshipPractices.name: Chandra Puja
worshipPractices.description: Worship of Chandra, often including Rohini as his consort, for marital harmony and emotional well-being.

festivals.name: Rohini Nakshatra Celebrations
festivals.timing: Occurs monthly when the moon is in Rohini Nakshatra
festivals.description: Observances and prayers are offered for prosperity and well-being.

cultural.modernLegacy: Rohini's name and story continue to inspire art, literature, and popular culture, symbolizing love, beauty, and auspiciousness. Her Nakshatra remains vital in Hindu astrology.
